stories.title: The Lost Kitten
stories.story:
One rainy afternoon, Omar was walking home from school when he heard a tiny sound coming from behind a wall. He stopped and looked closer. There, shivering in the rain, was a small orange kitten. It looked hungry, scared, and completely alone.

Omar knew he couldn't just leave it there. He carefully picked up the kitten and carried it home under his jacket, keeping it warm and dry. His mother was surprised to see him walk in with a tiny, wet visitor.

"Can we keep her?" Omar asked hopefully. His mother explained that they needed to find out if the kitten already had an owner first. Together, they made posters and asked all the neighbors, but nobody knew where the kitten had come from.

After a week with no answer, Omar's mother finally smiled. "I suppose she belongs with us now," she said. Omar was thrilled. He named the kitten Lucky, because that's exactly what she was — lucky to have found a warm home, and lucky to have found Omar.

stories.title: The Talent Show
stories.story:
Every year, Nour's school held a talent show, and every year, Nour watched from the audience, too shy to join. She loved to sing, but the thought of standing on a stage in front of everyone made her stomach hurt.

This year, her friend Dina wouldn't stop asking. "You have such a beautiful voice, Nour. Please just try!" Finally, Nour agreed, mostly just to make Dina stop asking. She signed up for a spot, and immediately regretted it.

For two weeks, Nour practiced her song every single day, sometimes in front of her mirror, sometimes in front of her little brother, who clapped no matter how she sounded. Slowly, the fear started to feel a little smaller.

On the day of the show, Nour's hands were shaking as she walked on stage. But when the music started, something changed. She closed her eyes and just sang, the way she always did at home. When she finished, the whole room clapped loudly. Nour opened her eyes, smiling. She hadn't just sung a song — she had discovered she was braver than she thought.
